list.text: Technical Architect
PHP & Laravel Expert

box.title: Aravind Govindhasamy

list.text: Less Code: Fewer files in the default installation.
Streamlined: Simplified service providers and middleware.

box.title: Laravel 11 reduces boilerplate by moving configuration out of the application and into the framework core.

list.text: bootstrap/app.php: The new hub for routing and middleware.
config/: Fully optional - default values are now integrated.

box.title: A cleaner organization for modern PHP applications.

list.text: Zero Config: Start building immediately.
Fast: Perfect for rapid prototyping and testing.

box.title: Laravel 11 now uses SQLite as the default database, making local development even faster.

slides.title: 2. Bridging the Gap
slides.subtitle: How Inertia connects Laravel to modern frontend frameworks.

list.text: The Bridge: It sits between your server-side framework and your client-side framework.
No API Required: You don't need to build a REST or GraphQL API.

box.title: Inertia allows you to create fully client-side rendered, single-page apps, using classic server-side routing and controllers.

list.text: Header-based: The server detects the header and returns JSON instead of HTML.
State Update: The client-side framework receives the JSON and updates the UI.

box.title: Inertia works by intercepting standard link clicks and sending an XHR request with specific headers.

list.text: First Hit: Standard SSR for fast initial paint.
Navigation: Super fast JSON-only updates.

box.title: The first request returns full HTML with a root div, while subsequentnavigations return only data.

slides.title: 3. Elegant Backend Flow
slides.subtitle: Defining routes and controllers for Inertia.

box.title: Use the same `routes/web.php` file you've always used.
Render your frontend components directly from your PHP controllers.
Passing dynamic data through URLs to your components.
Using the Ziggy package to access your Laravel routes directly in your Vue or React components.

slides.title: 4. Sharing Information
slides.subtitle: Mastering Eloquent and global state sharing.

box.title: Transforming your database models into clean, predictable JSON for the frontend.
Sharing data (like the authenticated user) with every Inertia response automatically.
Optimize performance by only sending expensive data when it's explicitly requested by the frontend.

list.text: Ready-to-go: Pre-configured with Inertia and Vue/React.
Tailwind: Styled with Tailwind CSS out of the box.

box.title: A minimal, simple implementation of all of Laravel's authentication features, including login, registration, password reset, and dashboard.
Providing a simple, uniform way to authorize user actions against your models.
Laravel sends authorization status to the frontend via shared data, allowing you to toggle UI elements securely.

list.text: Auto-token: CSRF tokens are handled automatically by Inertia/Axios.
Helmet: Easy integration of security headers.

box.title: Laravel's built-in protection against cross-site request forgery and other common attacks.

slides.title: 6. Modern Bundling
slides.subtitle: Mastering Vite in the Laravel ecosystem.

box.title: Vite provides a lightning-fast development experience with hot module replacement.

list.text: Unified: Same build process for client and server.
Performance: Fast initial paints with pre-rendered HTML.

box.title: Inertia uses Vite to bundle your components for server-side rendering.

list.text: Infrastructure: Automate server setup (Nginx, MySQL, PHP).
CI/CD: Deploy on push to your main branch.

box.title: Painless server management and deployment optimized specifically for Laravel.

list.text: Infinite Scale: Scale your app to millions of requests.
Cost Effective: Pay only for what you use.

box.title: The serverless deployment platform for Laravel, powered by AWS Lambda.

list.text: Sail: A lightweight CLI for interacting with Laravel's default Docker development environment.
Production: Multi-stage builds for lean PHP images.

box.title: Using Laravel Sail for local development and optimized Dockerfiles for production.

slides.title: 8. Complex UI Structures
slides.subtitle: Mastering persistent and nested layouts in Inertia.

box.title: Keep state (like playing audio or form input) as you navigate between pages.
Stacking layouts for deep application structures.
Detecting when a navigation is occurring to show loading states or progress bars.

slides.title: 9. Interactive Mutations
slides.subtitle: The Inertia form helper and validation flow.

box.code:
const form = useForm({ email: '', password: '' });
form.post('/login');
box.title: A powerful hook for managing form state, submission, and validation errors.
Laravel handles the validation, and Inertia automatically shares the error bag with your component.
Easily toggle button states and show success messages using form helper properties.

slides.title: 10. Living Applications
slides.subtitle: Adding real-time interactivity with Laravel Reverb.

list.text: Fast: Built for high throughput and low latency.
Integrated: Deeply integrated with Laravel Echo.

box.title: A first-party, high-performance WebSocket server specifically designed for Laravel.
Trigger real-time updates from your PHP backend by implementing the ShouldBroadcast interface.
Using Laravel Echo to listen for events and update your Inertia state.

slides.title: 11. Quality Assurance
slides.subtitle: Testing Laravel and Inertia with Pest.

box.title: A delightful PHP testing framework with a focus on simplicity and readability.
Asserting that the correct components and data are being returned from your controllers.

list.text: Cross-browser: Test across different rendering engines.
Robust: Handle asynchronous interactions with ease.

box.title: Ensuring the full user flow works correctly in the browser.

list.text: Velocity: Move from idea to production faster than any other stack.
Stability: A mature ecosystem with long-term support.
Experience: A delightful developer experience on both backend and frontend.

box.title: The most productive and mature way to build powerful, modern web applications.

list.text: Growth: Laravel continues to innovate at a rapid pace.
Tooling: New first-party tools are released regularly.

box.title: Stay updated with the latest in the Laravel ecosystem.

list.text: Build: Install Laravel with the Inertia starter kit.
Learn: Explore Laracasts and the official documentation.

box.title: Start your Laravel 11 journey today.
Thank you for your time! Any questions?
